# Contributing to Sunrize

First of all, thank you for your interest in contributing to **Sunrize**!
We welcome code improvements, bug reports, feature suggestions, documentation updates, and test cases.

This document outlines how to get involved.

---

## Getting Started

1. **Fork** the repository and clone your fork.
2. Install [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/) (LTS version recommended).
3. Install dependencies:

```sh
npm install
```

4. Run Sunrize in development mode on macOS or Linux:

```sh
npm run development
```

## Code Contributions

* Please follow the existing code style.
* Write descriptive commit messages.
* Ensure your code passes linting and basic tests.

## Suggested Improvements

* Improve performance and responsiveness when working with large or complex X3D scenes, especially during real-time editing and navigation.
* Enhance the user interface with more intuitive controls, customizable layouts, and clearer visual feedback for scene hierarchy and node selection.
 * Expand debugging and validation tools, including better error reporting, live scene inspection, and integrated support for X3D standards compliance.
* Add more advanced editing features, and improved import/export support for formats like glTF.

## Reporting Issues

* Please search existing issues before opening a new one.
* Include a minimal reproducible example if reporting a bug.
* Be clear about browser versions and platform if the bug is rendering-related.

## Improving Documentation

* Help us expand the [documentation](https://create3000.github.io/sunrize/documentation/overview/).
* Add code examples, explain X3D usage, or improve clarity.

## Pull Request Checklist

* [x] Clear, focused scope (one feature or fix per PR).
* [x] Runs without errors.
* [x] Includes tests if applicable.
* [x] You have signed off on your work (`git commit -s` is welcome but not required).

## License

By contributing, you agree that your contributions will be licensed under the same MIT License as the rest of the project.

---

Thank you for helping make Sunrize better!

The create3000 team
